steps.title: Combine both benefits
steps.content: The passage defines community science as collaboration between professional scientists and the public. It then gives two central benefits: the approach can build youth confidence in science, and it can greatly increase the amount of data researchers collect, as in the butterfly-color study.

steps.title: Choose the main idea
steps.content: Choice B includes the youth-confidence benefit but denies the data benefit. Choice C focuses too narrowly on Clara Benton's butterfly research and invents surprise about youth confidence. Choice D includes the data benefit but denies the youth-confidence benefit. Choice A includes both stated benefits, so it best summarizes why community science helps communities and researchers. The answer is A.
